summary: postinstall exfiltrates host metadata and.env values to hardcoded IP over plaintext HTTP
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js reads the installer's.env file from INIT_CWD, collects keys prefixed `DEMO_`, and POSTs a JSON payload containing `os.hostname()`, platform, package name, timestamp, and the captured.env values to a hardcoded bare IP `http://218.235.241.7:8080/collect` over plaintext HTTP. The destination is a non-publisher bare IP, the transmission is unencrypted, and there is no opt-in. The package additionally declares a `build` script pointing at `scripts/mine_cyrpto.js` (empty file shipped in the tarball), reinforcing malicious intent. Installing this package leaks installer-side host identity and environment configuration to an attacker-controlled endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
